book_claims.claim: Used in small amounts for cooking shellfish in the Longevity Diet.

supplementary_research.finding: White wine used in cooking retains polyphenols (tyrosol, caffeic acid) even after heating; alcohol content largely evaporates within 15 minutes of cooking.
Small amounts of white wine in cooking contribute negligible alcohol to the final dish and provide volatile aromatic compounds that enhance palatability.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Survives cooking; antioxidant structurally related to olive oil's hydroxytyrosol
Polyphenol retained after alcohol evaporation; anti-inflammatory in cell models
Act as flavor enhancers and tenderize shellfish proteins

synergies.description: Acidity of wine denatures shellfish proteins improving texture; enhances absorption of shellfish zinc and selenium
Allicin from garlic and tyrosol from wine together inhibit platelet aggregation and reduce cardiovascular risk
Wine acids and olive oil polyphenols form an emulsion improving flavor integration and phytonutrient delivery
